faculty: Faculté des sciences
program_description: Interdisciplinary master that trains professionals in science and marketing for the biochemical, pharmaceutical, cosmetic, food, paramedical, and biotechnology sectors.
admission_requirements: Bachelor's or Master's degree in biology, chemistry, or biochemistry; medical, pharmaceutical, or paramedical sciences including nutrition and medical laboratory analysis are also accepted; other degrees require USJ equivalence review; English proficiency is required; admission includes file evaluation and an interview.
interview_requirement: Interview with a selection committee is required after application review.
notes: Official program PDF; main language of instruction is French, English, and Arabic; campus listed as CST.

faculty: Faculté des sciences
notes: English-title listing on the graduate hub and French faculty page.

faculty: Faculté des sciences
program_description: Master's program that trains specialists in large-scale data collection, processing, analytics, and decision-support systems.
admission_requirements: Applicants to the first semester must hold a Bachelor of Engineering in Computer and Communications Engineering, Computer Science, or Mathematics, or an equivalent degree recognized by USJ. Applicants to the third semester must hold a Bachelor of Engineering in Computer and Communications Engineering, be a CCE student at ESIB with at least 120 credits in the engineering cycle, or hold a Master in Computer Science, Computer and Communications Engineering, Mathematics, or an equivalent degree recognized by USJ. Admission is based on the application file and an interview might be required.
notes: Official program PDF; main language of instruction is French, English, and Arabic; campus listed as CST.

faculty: Faculté des sciences
Faculté des sciences
department: Département de sciences de la vie et de la terre
official_degree_name: Master en sciences et gestion de l'environnement
program_description: Multidisciplinary master's program focused on understanding environmental issues holistically and training experts in analysis, pollution prevention, treatment, and governance.
admission_requirements: Applicants may hold a USJ licence in chemistry, geography, physics, life and earth sciences (biochemistry), laboratory sciences, or an engineering diploma. Holders of a licence or first-year scientific master's degree from outside USJ may be admitted if the degree is judged equivalent by the USJ equivalence commission.
notes: Official program PDF; main language of instruction is French, English, and Arabic; campus listed as CST.
